title: [Ninetales] Battle Guide
summary: A [Fire]-type special attacker whose [drought] ability sets sun automatically — the anchor of any sun-based team composition.
strengths: Speed 100 outruns most of the mid-upper metagame
[nasty-plot] converts the [support] role into sweeper damage
weaknesses: Loses the win condition when sun is erased

recommendedMoves.reason: Setup move that doubles SpA for the sweep

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Ninetales] is a pure-[Fire] special attacker whose identity revolves a[round] its hidden ability [drought] — it sets sun on entry, anchoring any sun-based team composition. In Champions, it pairs directly with [chlorophyll] sweepers like Venusaur, and its own offense is strong enough to sweep when [nasty-plot] is active.

## Strengths
[drought] is the root of the team plan. Under sun, [flamethrower] damage goes up by 50% and [solar-beam] becomes a one-turn move, which gives [Ninetales] a full cove[rage] kit from a [sing]le slot. Speed 100 outruns most of the mid-upper metagame, and [nasty-plot] converts the [support] role into a real finisher. [will-o-wisp] is a bonus utility slot that weakens physical attackers to preserve bulk.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Rock], [Water], and [Ground] weaknesses are all common cove[rage], and [stealth-rock] chips 25% on every entry. 73/75/100 bulk leaves no room for setup [mist]akes, and the whole team plan collapses when oppo[sing] weather — rain, sand, snow — overwrites sun. The team must carry a dedicated answer to oppo[sing] weather setters to preserve the plan.

## Recommended Moveset
[flamethrower] is the locked STAB for consistent sun-boosted damage. [solar-beam] becomes a one-turn finisher under sun, providing mandatory cove[rage] for [Water], [Ground], and [Rock] switch-ins. [nasty-plot] is the setup slot that turns [Ninetales] into a sweeper, and [will-o-wisp] is the utility slot for long-game physical attacker weakening. A [hex] variant that pairs burn with [Ghost] damage is a niche alternative.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[heat-rock] is the default item because it extends sun from five to eight turns, which is the difference between a [sing]le sweep and a full team plan. [leftovers] is the self-sufficient alternative, and [focus-sash] provides setup insurance. Ability must be [drought] — [flash]-[fire] is only a niche pick. Tera [Fire] maxes [flamethrower] damage and Tera [Grass] boosts [solar-beam] for a decisive cove[rage] pick.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Ninetales] exists to enable [chlorophyll] partners like Venusaur — that pairing is effectively automatic. [Ground] or [Grass] partners cover the [Rock] and [Water] weaknesses, and a [taunt] lead or pivot line answers oppo[sing] weather setters. The team also needs a dedicated hazard remover because [Ninetales] hates [stealth-rock] chip on every entry.

## Play Patterns
Lead [Ninetales] to activate [drought], then either pivot out to bring in a [chlorophyll] sweeper or stay in for a [nasty-plot] sweep if the matchup is safe. Use [will-o-wisp] on any physical attacker entering the field, and Tera on a telegraphed [Rock] or [Water] move.
